team_context.cap_situation: flexible — ~$37M in space after aggressive free agency spending, but Crosby's $35.8M cap hit returning unexpectedly after failed Ravens trade complicates the picture. Had $111M+ entering FA week.
team_context.trade_posture: Sellers on veterans, buyers of draft capital. The Raiders went 3-14 in 2025, hold the #1 overall pick (expected to be QB Fernando Mendoza), and are in a full rebuild under new HC Klint Kubiak and GM John Spytek. They tried to trade Maxx Crosby for two 1sts but the deal collapsed due to a failed physical. They will aggressively seek trade partners for Crosby and could move other veterans for picks.

players.name: Aidan O'Connell
players.performance_evidence: Career backup/spot starter. In 2023 started 10 games with 2,200+ yards and 12 TDs. Was behind Geno Smith in 2025 and started only when Smith was hurt. Adequate arm, limited mobility, below-average starter ceiling. Raiders are expected to draft Fernando Mendoza #1 overall — O'Connell projects as the QB2.

trade_value.reasoning: Backup QB on a cheap final-year rookie deal. Provides value as an experienced placeholder/bridge QB for a team that needs a stop-gap, but limited upside caps trade interest. Worth a late-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Raiders are drafting Mendoza #1 overall. O'Connell becomes the veteran backup. He could be moved if a team has a QB injury, but his trade value is minimal. More likely to stay as the bridge/backup.

players.name: Ashton Jeanty
players.performance_evidence: Rookie season: 975 rushing yards on 266 carries (3.7 YPC), 5 rushing TDs in 17 games. Numbers suppressed by the NFL's worst offensive line (64 sacks allowed) and anemic offense (14.2 PPG, 32nd). Flashed elite ability — 188 yards and 2 TDs vs Houston in Week 16, showed elite contact balance and broken tackle rate (2nd among RBs). PFF overall grade of 70.4. Heisman runner-up in college with 2,601 rushing yards.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old former top-6 pick with 4 years of rookie deal control and elite college production. Rookie stats were suppressed by historically bad OL play. RB position value is low, but the age/contract combo and pedigree give him a late-1st/early-2nd equivalent. Dead cap of $22.7M makes a trade unlikely anyway.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the rebuild. The Raiders invested the 6th overall pick in him and are building around Jeanty, Bowers, and their incoming franchise QB. Massive dead cap ($22.7M) makes a trade financially impractical regardless.

players.name: Dylan Laube
players.performance_evidence: Versatile pass-catching back who earned a role as a 6th-round pick. Limited rushing production but contributes as a receiver out of the backfield and on special teams. Complementary piece behind Jeanty.

trade_value.reasoning: Late-round backup RB on a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value — a team would need to give up only a conditional 7th to acquire him, and even that's generous.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece with minimal value. Raiders would move him for any draft pick but nobody is calling.

players.name: Tre Tucker
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 57 receptions, 696 yards, 5 TDs in 17 games — all career highs. Led the team in receiving yards after Jakobi Meyers was traded. Deep threat with 4.40 speed but only 5'8/182 lbs. Set career highs in targets (92), receptions, yards, and TDs. OTC valuation of $17.875M shows he's significantly outplaying his rookie deal, but production was boosted by lack of competition in the WR room.

trade_value.reasoning: Final year of a cheap rookie deal with decent production. Expiring contract caps value — acquiring team gets one year of a WR2/3-caliber player before he hits free agency. Worth a 4th-round pick. Speedster profile limits his ceiling as a true WR1.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering his final contract year. Raiders signed Jalen Nailor and could draft WRs. If Tucker doesn't sign an extension, he's a trade candidate before the deadline. Team may prefer to extend him, but he's not untouchable.

players.name: Jalen Nailor
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 3-year, $35M deal with $23M guaranteed as a free agent from the Vikings. In Minnesota, he was primarily a slot receiver and special teams contributor, with limited opportunity behind Justin Jefferson and Jordan Addison. Career stats are modest — breakout potential exists but is unproven. Nickname 'Speedy' with good instincts for finding open spots.

trade_value.reasoning: Just signed a $35M deal — high dead cap ($18M) makes a trade financially impractical in year 1. Even if tradeable, he's unproven as a starter at this price point. Worth a mid-4th in a vacuum but the dead cap prevents any trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a key free agent addition to build around the new franchise QB. Dead cap of $18M in year 1 makes a trade virtually impossible. The Raiders want to see what he can do in their offense first.

players.name: Jack Bech
players.performance_evidence: 2025 2nd-round pick who contributed as a rookie but was inconsistent. Physical receiver with size, but struggled with separation at the NFL level as a rookie. Still developing — upside remains given his draft pedigree and age.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 2nd-round pick on a cheap rookie deal with 4 years of control. Despite an underwhelming rookie year, the age/contract/pedigree profile still commands a mid-3rd to early-4th round equivalent. Raiders drafted him — unlikely to give up on him after one year.

players.availability_reasoning: Current regime drafted Bech in the 2nd round — they believe in him. Would need a clear overpay (2nd-round pick) to move a player they just invested significant draft capital in.
players.injury_notes: Missed some time with a back injury in 2025.

players.name: Brock Bowers
players.performance_evidence: 2024 (rookie): 112 receptions, 1,194 yards, 5 TDs — set NFL records for most receptions by a rookie and most receiving yards by a rookie TE. Named First-Team All-Pro and Pro Bowl as a rookie. 2025: 64 receptions, 680 yards, 7 TDs in 12 games before being placed on IR with a knee injury (PCL/bone bruise). Even in a limited season on a 3-14 team with terrible QB play, he was the Raiders' best offensive weapon. Generational TE talent.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old All-Pro TE on a rookie deal with 2 years remaining plus a 5th-year option. This is one of the most valuable non-QB assets in the entire NFL. His combination of age, production, contract, and position scarcity places him in the range of multiple 1st-round picks. Only the very best QBs on rookie deals are more valuable.

players.availability_reasoning: Absolute franchise cornerstone. Set rookie records, named All-Pro at age 21, still on a cheap rookie deal. The Raiders will build their entire offense around Bowers and the incoming franchise QB. There is no realistic offer that would pry him loose.
players.injury_notes: Suffered PCL injury and bone bruise in Week 1 of 2025, played through it but was eventually placed on IR in Week 16. Knee injury is concerning but expected to recover fully for 2026.

players.name: Michael Mayer
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 35 receptions, 328 yards, 1 TD in 13 games. Career totals: 83 receptions, 788 yards across 3 seasons. Solid blocker but has never developed as a receiving threat — permanently overshadowed by Brock Bowers. Best game came in Week 17 vs Giants (9-89-0) when Bowers was on IR. Former 2nd-round pick who hasn't lived up to pedigree as a pass catcher.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring deal on a blocking TE who hasn't developed as a receiver despite 2nd-round pedigree. Worth a late 5th at best given the final year and limited production. A contender needing a blocking TE might have interest.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his rookie deal. Bowers is firmly the TE1 and Mayer hasn't earned an extension. Raiders would move him for any pick of value. Not being actively shopped but has a clear price.
players.injury_notes: Ankle injury caused him to miss time in 2025.

players.name: Kolton Miller
players.performance_evidence: 7-year starter who has appeared in 109 games (107 starts). Signed a 3-year, $66M extension in 2025. Durable blindside protector at 6'8, 325 lbs — has been the Raiders' most reliable offensive lineman for years. Still playing at a solid level at age 29 in 2025, playing all 17 games. Not elite anymore but a dependable above-average LT.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting LT with 2 years remaining on a fair-market deal ($22M AAV). Age (30) limits upside, but LTs are premium and he's still productive. Worth a mid-to-late 2nd to a contender needing LT help. $6.7M dead cap is manageable.

players.availability_reasoning: The Raiders just extended him in 2025 and he's been their OL anchor. With a rebuilding team, there's a world where they'd move him for a strong draft pick package, but he's also exactly the kind of veteran you want protecting a rookie QB. Would take a 2nd-round pick to pry him loose.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder issue caused him to miss 4 games in 2023, but has been healthy since. Played all 17 games in both 2024 and 2025.

players.name: Atonio Mafi
players.performance_evidence: Entering his 4th year. Has started games at guard for the Raiders but has been inconsistent. Below-average pass blocker who has struggled with the transition to the NFL. Part of the OL unit that allowed a league-worst 64 sacks in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starter on an expiring deal. Minimal trade value — essentially a replacement-level player at this point. A conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Raiders signed Linderbaum and have JPJ, plus could draft OL. Mafi is expendable and the team would move him for anything of value.

players.name: Tyler Linderbaum
players.performance_evidence: Three consecutive Pro Bowls (2023-25) as the Ravens' center. Second-best pass block win rate among centers (96.2%) since being drafted. PFF offensive grade of 80.3 in 2025 (5th among centers). Started 71 of 73 possible games in his career. Now the highest-paid interior lineman in NFL history at $27M AAV. Anchored the Ravens' league-best run game.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite young center on a new 3-year deal. Would command a 1st-round pick in a vacuum, but the $60M in guarantees and $40M dead cap make a trade financially impossible. This is a cornerstone signing for the rebuild.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a record-breaking $81M deal to be the centerpiece of the Raiders' rebuilt OL. $60M in guarantees and $40M dead cap in 2026 make a trade impossible. He's here to protect the franchise QB.

players.name: Jackson Powers-Johnson
players.performance_evidence: 2024: 15 games, 14 starts (LG and C), named PFWA All-Rookie Team. 2025: 8 appearances, 7 starts at RG before ankle injury in Week 10 ended season (placed on IR). PFF ranked 37th of 81 qualifying guards — upper half. Won Rimington Trophy in college as nation's top center. Moved to guard with Linderbaum signing. Only 23 years old with significant upside despite rocky 2nd season.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old 2nd-round pick on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years remaining. Despite inconsistency in Year 2, the age/contract profile is excellent. All-Rookie honors in Year 1. Worth a 3rd-round pick. ESPN reported Raiders 'don't plan to trade him' after the Linderbaum signing.

players.availability_reasoning: Reports indicate the Raiders plan to keep JPJ at guard alongside Linderbaum. However, he struggled in 2025 and the previous coaching staff seemed down on him. New HC Kubiak may have different plans. Would take a strong offer (2nd-round pick) to move a 23-year-old with upside.
players.injury_notes: Ankle injury ended his 2025 season in Week 10 (placed on IR).
players.trade_buzz: During 2025 season, there was speculation about trading JPJ after he was benched. ESPN's Jeremy Fowler reported after Linderbaum signing that Raiders 'don't plan to trade him.'

players.name: DJ Glaze
players.performance_evidence: 2024 3rd-round pick who started at RT as a rookie. Struggled significantly — ranked as the 3rd-worst OT in sacks allowed per Next Gen Stats. Part of the league's worst pass-blocking unit. Still developing at 24 years old but has significant room to grow.

trade_value.reasoning: Young developmental OT on a cheap rookie deal. The struggles are real but he's only 24 with 2 years of control. Worth a late 4th/early 5th — a team with a good OL coach might see a project worth investing in.

players.availability_reasoning: Raiders are looking to upgrade at RT — Glaze was one of the worst pass-blocking OTs in the league. If they draft a tackle or sign one, Glaze becomes available. But the cost to acquire him is low, so there's no rush to move him.

players.name: Thomas Booker IV
players.performance_evidence: Interior defensive lineman in a 3-4 scheme. Has shown some flashes as a run stuffer but has limited pass-rush upside. Depth piece who has started some games by necessity on a talent-depleted defense.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring deal on a rotational DL. Essentially replacement level. Minimal trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth DL on a rebuilding team. Would be moved for any pick of value.

players.name: Adam Butler
players.performance_evidence: Veteran interior pass rusher who has been a solid contributor for the Raiders. Decent interior pressure numbers but declining as he enters his 30s. Good locker room presence and leadership.

trade_value.reasoning: 31-year-old veteran DT on an expiring deal. Worth a late 5th to a contender needing interior pass rush depth. Age and contract situation severely limit value.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran on a rebuilding team with an expiring deal. Classic trade deadline candidate — could provide interior pressure for a contender at minimal cost.

players.name: Jonah Laulu
players.performance_evidence: 2024 5th-round pick who showed promise as a rotational DE in his rookie season. Earned increased snaps and showed up in the run game. Still developing as a pass rusher. One of the few young defensive pieces with upside.

trade_value.reasoning: Young rotational DL on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years remaining. Developing prospect with modest upside. Worth a 6th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, developing player — exactly the type the Raiders should keep during a rebuild. Not being shopped.

players.name: Maxx Crosby
players.performance_evidence: Five-time Pro Bowler, two-time All-Pro. 2025: 10 sacks and career-high 28 TFL in 15 games before being shut down with knee injury. Has reached double-digit sacks 4 times in 7 seasons. Third in franchise history in sacks (69.5). 6th in NFL in sacks since 2019. One of the premier pass rushers in the NFL when healthy.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite pass rusher entering his age-29 season with 4 years/$113M remaining. The Ravens valued him at two 1st-round picks before the failed physical torpedoed the deal. Post-physical-failure, his value has taken a significant hit — knee concerns (meniscus surgery in January) will scare some teams. Realistically worth a mid-1st rounder now, down from two 1sts. The $5.1M dead cap if traded is very manageable for LV.

players.availability_reasoning: The Raiders agreed to trade him to Baltimore for two 1sts — that tells you everything. The team is rebuilding, Crosby doesn't fit the timeline, and they want draft capital. The failed physical complicated things, but the Raiders will still try to move him. His value has dropped due to knee concerns and the $29M 2027 salary that became fully guaranteed on March 13. Teams will still call, but the price has come down.
players.injury_notes: CRITICAL: Played through a torn meniscus in his left knee throughout much of 2025, was shut down for final 2 games. Had meniscus repair surgery in January 2026. Failed physical with the Ravens in March 2026, causing the trade to collapse. There are concerns about arthritis and long-term wear. This is the single biggest factor affecting his trade value.
players.trade_buzz: Agreed to trade to Ravens for 2 first-round picks (2026 #14 overall + 2027 1st) on March 8. Ravens backed out March 11 after failed physical due to knee concerns. Multiple teams (Bears, Eagles, others) were interested before the Ravens deal. Raiders 'won't trade Crosby on a discount' per SI's Albert Breer, but his market has shrunk. Raiders are 'prepared to get Crosby healthy and have him play' for 2026 if no adequate offer materializes.

players.name: Nakobe Dean
players.performance_evidence: 2024 with Eagles: 128 tackles, 3 sacks, forced fumble, INT in 15 games — breakout season, earned All-Pro votes. 2025: 4 sacks, 55 tackles, 2 forced fumbles in 10 games before hamstring injury. Best blitzing LB in the league per multiple analysts. Athletic, physical, productive when healthy. Super Bowl champion with Philly.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old productive LB on a fresh 3-year deal. Excellent blitzing ability and coverage skills. High dead cap ($15M) in year 1 makes a trade financially impractical. In a vacuum, he'd command a 3rd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a cornerstone free agent acquisition. Dead cap of $15M in year 1 makes a trade virtually impossible. Raiders invested heavily to pair him with Quay Walker.
players.injury_notes: Significant injury history: Lisfranc injury ended 2023 season after 5 games. Tore patellar tendon in January 2025 playoff game. Hamstring injury in late 2025. Durability is a real concern — has played a full 17-game season only once (never).

players.name: Quay Walker
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick who topped 100 tackles in all 4 Packers seasons. 2025: 128 tackles (career high), 2.5 sacks, 8 TFL, 4 PBU. Elite athleticism with sideline-to-sideline range. Weakness: liability in coverage, inconsistent mental processing. Good run defender but hasn't lived up to 1st-round billing overall. PFF ranked 19th among off-ball LBs.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old former 1st-round pick with elite athletic traits and consistent tackle production. New 3-year/$40.5M contract with $28M guaranteed and $20M dead cap makes a year-1 trade impossible. Worth a 3rd-round pick in a vacuum.

players.availability_reasoning: Brand new signing with massive dead cap. Raiders just invested $40.5M to pair him with Dean. Zero chance of a trade in 2026.
players.character_notes: Has had discipline issues — fined for roughing the passer, had a frustration penalty early in his career with Green Bay.

players.name: Malcolm Koonce
players.performance_evidence: 2023: Career-best 8 sacks and 17 QB hits. Missed entire 2024 season with torn ACL. 2025: Returned to play, recorded 4.5 sacks and 30 tackles — didn't replicate pre-injury production but showed flashes. Good in the run game. On a 1-year prove-it deal. Fits the 3-4 scheme under DC Rob Leonard, his former position coach.

trade_value.reasoning: Coming off ACL, on a 1-year deal. If he plays well early, he could be a trade deadline piece for a contender — worth a 4th-round pick to a team needing edge help. But the 1-year deal and injury history limit value.

players.availability_reasoning: On a 1-year prove-it deal. If Crosby stays and the team adds edge rushers in the draft, Koonce could be flipped at the trade deadline for a pick. Classic rental candidate.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in 2024, missed entire season. Returned in 2025 but wasn't at full strength. Two years removed from the injury in 2026, expected to be healthier.

players.name: Darien Porter
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie: 29 solo tackles, 3 PD in 17 games. PFF overall grade of 54.4 (90th of 114 CBs) — struggled significantly as a rookie. 6'2/195, rare size and 4.3 speed but was a WR-to-CB convert at Iowa State with limited starting experience. Drafted for tools/upside, not immediate production. Pete Carroll compared him to Richard Sherman as a development project.

trade_value.reasoning: Young CB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Poor rookie PFF grade but has elite physical tools (6'2, 4.3 speed) and was a 3rd-round pick. A team betting on development could see value. Worth a mid-4th.

players.availability_reasoning: Current regime drafted him and sees him as a long-term development piece. Won't move him cheap. Would need a significant overpay given the physical tools and cheap contract.

players.name: Eric Stokes
players.performance_evidence: 2025 with Raiders: 53 tackles, 5 PBU in 16 starts. 18th-highest graded CB per PFF (73.6 overall grade). Limited QBs to 54.5% completion rate and 85.3 passer rating when targeted. One of the bright spots on a bad defense. Former 1st-round pick who bounced back after injury-plagued years in Green Bay (missed 23 games 2022-24).

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starting CB on a new 3-year/$30M deal. Just re-signed — dead cap of $15M makes a year-1 trade impossible. In a vacuum, a quality CB2 at $10M AAV with 3 years remaining commands a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed to anchor the secondary. $15M dead cap in year 1 prevents any trade. Raiders invested in him as their CB1/CB2 for the rebuild.
players.injury_notes: History of injuries in Green Bay — missed 23 games from 2022-2024. Stayed healthy in 2025 (16 starts), which is encouraging. Durability remains a watch item.

players.name: Taron Johnson
players.performance_evidence: Veteran nickel corner acquired from the Bills for a pick swap (6th for 7th). Spent 7 seasons in Buffalo as a reliable slot defender. Has experience in multiple defensive schemes and is a sure tackler in the slot. Raiders needed nickel help badly — Taron fills that void as a proven NFL slot corner.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran nickel on a 1-year deal. Reliable but not elite. Worth a late 5th/early 6th. Could be a deadline flip if a contender loses their nickel to injury.

players.availability_reasoning: Just acquired, but on a 1-year deal. If the Raiders are out of contention (likely), he's a trade deadline candidate for a contender needing nickel help.
players.trade_buzz: Acquired from Buffalo on March 8 for a pick swap (sent 6th-round pick, received 7th-round pick).

players.name: Jeremy Chinn
players.performance_evidence: 2025 with Raiders: Solid production as the starting SS — roughly 95 tackles, 3 TFL, 3 PBU, 1 FF through the full season. 2024 with Commanders: 117 tackles, 7 TFL, 2 sacks, 1 INT, 5 PBU, 2 FF. Versatile chess piece at 6'3/220 who can play in the box, at free safety, or as a slot defender. Steve Atwater's nephew. Consistently productive if not flashy.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring deal on a versatile safety entering age-29 season. Quality starter with scheme versatility. Worth a mid-to-late 3rd to a contender needing safety help. Dead cap is only $2M, making a trade very clean.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal on a rebuilding team. Classic trade deadline candidate — productive veteran safety that a contender would love. Raiders would happily take a 3rd-round pick for him. Beat writers have suggested an extension is possible, but the rebuild timeline doesn't favor keeping aging vets.

players.name: Isaiah Pola-Mao
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who has worked his way into the starting FS role. 6'4/205, nephew of Troy Polamalu. Has shown some range and ball skills but is limited overall. Holds the starting job more by default on a talent-depleted roster than by merit. Below-average starter at this point.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA starting by default on a bad defense. Essentially no trade value. Replacement-level player.

players.availability_reasoning: Raiders would upgrade at FS if possible. Pola-Mao holds the spot by default, not merit. Would be moved for any pick.
